must_have_breakdown.skill: System Requirements writing and System Architecture Design
RADAR System (End User function) and Product knowledge
Experience on system service function (Diagnostics, Flashability, FOTA, etc) development
Basic Functional Safety and Cyber Security knowledge
Experience in RADAR function validation (HIL and vehicle environment)
Requirement management tools DOORs, Code Beamer and Configuration management tools such as CoreALM

certifications_assessed.name: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure AI Foundations 2025 Associate
Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Certified Foundations Associate
IT Specialist – Data Analytics (Certiport / Pearson VUE)

reasoning: Reject — wrong domain entirely. This candidate is a software/web developer and MCA student with no intersection with automotive radar systems engineering. Zero of 12 must-have skills are met — no ASPICE, no ISO26262, no RADAR domain knowledge, no embedded systems, no CAN/LIN/Ethernet protocols, no functional safety, no HIL validation, no DOORs or Catia Magic. The profile is centered on Python, FastAPI, React, and AI/ML for web applications. Even the academic background (MCA, BCA) is misaligned — this role requires automotive systems engineering experience. The email address on the resume matches the client email posting the job, suggesting this may be a test submission or accidental self-submission. Do not proceed — reject immediately.
strengths: PYTHON AND AI/ML CAPABILITY: Demonstrates hands-on AI/ML work including a CNN-based speech enhancement system using PyTorch and Whisper ASR, with end-to-end pipeline development — irrelevant to this radar systems role but shows genuine technical depth in a different domain.

MICROSERVICES AND BACKEND DEVELOPMENT: Built a 13-container microservices environment with FastAPI, Qdrant, and Gemini API at Fidelitus Corp, showing architectural thinking — but entirely in web/cloud context, not embedded or automotive.

COMPETITIVE CODING RECOGNITION: Global Rank ~1600 in TCS CodeVita Season 11 Round 2 signals strong problem-solving ability — a transferable trait but insufficient to bridge the domain gap for a senior radar systems role.
gaps: ZERO AUTOMOTIVE/RADAR DOMAIN EXPERIENCE: The role requires senior-level RADAR system function ownership including HIL validation, vehicle-level testing, ASPICE compliance, and ISO26262 functional safety. The candidate has no evidence of any automotive, embedded, or radar work anywhere in their history — this is a complete domain mismatch, not a gap that can be bridged.

NO EMBEDDED SYSTEMS OR AUTOMOTIVE PROTOCOLS KNOWLEDGE: CAN, LIN, Ethernet (automotive), LVDS, diagnostics, FOTA, and flashability are all mandatory. The candidate's entire stack is web-based (React, FastAPI, MongoDB) with no embedded or hardware-adjacent experience — this is disqualifying at the foundational level.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period stated; candidate appears to be a student with an internship ending June 2026, so no penalty applied.
JD requires in-office presence in Chennai; candidate is based in Bengaluru.
JD requires 4 years relevant experience; candidate has zero relevant automotive/radar experience and is currently pursuing an MCA degree.
JD gender preference is 'Any' so no penalty applies.
No college preference or exclusion specified in the JD.

availability_reason: Current internship at Fidelitus Corp ends June 2026 and candidate is an MCA student — effectively available immediately for a full-time role after that date.
alternative_role_fit: Junior Python/AI Developer or Junior Backend Engineer
self_reported_unverified: Selected by college for technical capability and reliability to work on the College ERP application — no verifying details such as scope, users impacted, or supervisor confirmation.
Core member of the JobOS product team at Fidelitus Corp — intern tenure starts April 2026 (future-dated at time of evaluation), making actual contribution unverifiable.
interview_flags: Probe why this candidate applied for a senior automotive radar systems role given zero domain overlap — possible accidental or test submission given the email matches the client who posted the JD.
Fidelitus Corp internship is dated April 2026 to June 2026 — this is a future role at the time of most evaluations; confirm whether this is accurate or a data entry error.
Ask whether candidate has any exposure to embedded C, AUTOSAR, or automotive protocols through coursework or self-study not mentioned in the resume.
Clarify actual scope of the DSU ERP Automation Module — 'selected by college' is a strong claim for a student; verify deliverables and real-world deployment status.
Confirm whether the candidate understands the nature of this role (automotive radar systems engineering at senior level) before investing any further recruiter time.
